message:
refactor(error): replace bail!() with structured errors (bd-2f8g progress)

- Remove 5 stale ignored tests for non-existent error variants (bd-3b94)
- Update error code range docs to match implementation
- Replace 7 bail!() calls with structured error types:
  - DaemonError::SocketNotFound (2 instances)
  - SshError::BinaryNotFound (1 instance)
  - SshError::ToolchainInstallFailed (1 instance)
  - ConfigError::InvalidValue (3 instances)

All tests pass. ~28 bail!() calls remain for future work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2f5o","title":"WS3: Replace RwLock with Atomics for Small Types in WorkerState","description":"PROBLEM: rchd/src/workers.rs WorkerState (lines 17-40) wraps 10 fields in RwLock:
  Line 19: config: RwLock<WorkerConfig>
  Line 21: status: RwLock<WorkerStatus>
  Line 25: speed_score: RwLock<f64>
  Line 27: last_latency_ms: RwLock<Option<u64>>
  Line 29: cached_projects: RwLock<Vec<String>>
  Line 31: circuit: RwLock<CircuitStats>
  Line 33: last_error_msg: RwLock<Option<String>>
  Line 35: capabilities: RwLock<WorkerCapabilities>
  Line 37: disabled_reason: RwLock<Option<String>>
  Line 39: disabled_at: RwLock<Option<i64>>

Only used_slots (line 23) already uses AtomicU32.

RwLock has ~25ns overhead per acquisition even uncontended. Worker selection reads speed_score, last_latency_ms, consecutive_failures, and total_builds for every worker during scoring.

WHY IT MATTERS: With N workers, selection does N * K lock acquisitions. AtomicU64 load with Relaxed ordering is ~1ns (single MOV on x86_64).

SOLUTION: Convert ONLY the hot-path scalar fields to atomics:
  - speed_score: RwLock<f64> → AtomicU64 (f64::to_bits/from_bits)
  - last_latency_ms: RwLock<Option<u64>> → AtomicU64 (0 = None)
  - disabled_at: RwLock<Option<i64>> → AtomicI64 (0 = None)

KEEP AS RWLOCK (complex types, not hot-path, or infrequently accessed):
  - config: RwLock<WorkerConfig> (complex struct, rarely changes)
  - status: RwLock<WorkerStatus> (enum with semantic transitions, needs write guard for TOCTOU fix)
  - cached_projects: RwLock<Vec<String>> (heap-allocated collection)
  - circuit: RwLock<CircuitStats> (multi-field struct)
  - last_error_msg: RwLock<Option<String>> (heap-allocated, diagnostic only)
  - capabilities: RwLock<WorkerCapabilities> (complex struct, set once)
  - disabled_reason: RwLock<Option<String>> (heap-allocated, diagnostic only)

NOTE: The original bead listed consecutive_failures and total_builds as RwLock fields, but they don't appear in the actual struct. Verify during implementation whether they exist elsewhere or were already atomic.

FILES: rchd/src/workers.rs (WorkerState struct + accessors), rchd/src/selection.rs (reads during scoring)
RISK: Medium — must ensure memory ordering correctness for f64 bit-casting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:36:31.559122805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T03:58:24.212899130Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T03:58:24.212829671Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks complete: WS3.1 (speed_score AtomicU64) CLOSED, WS3.2 (scalar RwLock replacements) CLOSED, WS3.3 (memory ordering verification) CLOSED, WS3.4 (unit tests) CLOSED. Three fields converted from RwLock to atomic: speed_score (AtomicU64 with f64 bit-casting), last_latency_ms (AtomicU64 with 0 sentinel), disabled_at (AtomicI64 with 0 sentinel).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2f5o","depends_on_id":"bd-2mmt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:45Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":12,"issue_id":"bd-2f5o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BACKGROUND: Worker selection iterates all N workers and reads speed_score, last_latency_ms, consecutive_failures, and total_builds for scoring. With RwLock, that is N * 4 lock acquisitions minimum per selection. Even uncontended, RwLock costs ~25ns per acquire+release (futex syscall on Linux). With 10 workers, that is 10 * 4 * 25ns = 1μs just in lock overhead.

AtomicU64 load with Relaxed ordering compiles to a single MOV instruction on x86_64 — effectively free (~1ns). This reduces lock overhead from ~1μs to ~40ns for the same 10-worker scenario.

CAVEAT: f64 is not natively atomic in Rust's std. The standard pattern is AtomicU64 + f64::to_bits/from_bits, which is a zero-cost transmute (same bits, different interpretation). This is well-established in the Rust ecosystem.

KEEP AS RWLOCK: last_health_check (Instant is 16 bytes, not atomic-friendly, and only accessed during periodic health checks — not on the hot path).","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:39:19Z"},{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-2f5o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CORRECTION FROM REVIEW: The original bead listed only 5 RwLock fields. The actual WorkerState struct has 10 RwLock fields plus 1 AtomicU32 (used_slots). After analysis, only 3 fields are candidates for atomic conversion (speed_score, last_latency_ms, disabled_at) — the rest are complex types (Vec, struct, Option<String>) that genuinely need RwLock.

Also: consecutive_failures and total_builds were listed in the original audit but do NOT exist as standalone fields in WorkerState. They may be tracked inside CircuitStats. This must be verified during implementation — do NOT create phantom fields.

The net impact is smaller than originally estimated (3 fields, not 5), but still worthwhile: those 3 fields are the ones read on every selection decision.","created_at":"2026-01-29T22:54:30Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2f8g","title":"Replace all bare bail\!() calls with structured errors","description":"# Replace bail\!() with Structured Errors

## Problem
There are ~35 `bail\!()` calls in commands.rs that produce user-unfriendly errors:

### Examples of Bad Errors
```rust
// Line 2167 - No context, no code, no remediation
bail\!("rch-wkr not found on remote");

// Line 2197 - Missing which worker
bail\!("rustup install failed: {}", stderr.trim());

// Line 2943 - No error code for scripting
bail\!("rustup install failed: {}", stderr.trim());

// Line 7317 - Generic message
bail\!("Daemon socket not found at {:?}", socket_path);
```

### What Users See
```
Error: rch-wkr not found on remote
```
No code to look up, no suggestion, no context.

## Solution
Replace with proper error types from rch/src/error.rs:

```rust
// Before
bail\!("rch-wkr not found on remote");

// After  
return Err(WorkerError::BinaryNotFound {
    worker_id: worker.id.to_string(),
    host: worker.host.clone(),
    binary: "rch-wkr".to_string(),
}.into());
```

## New Error Variants Needed

### WorkerError (add to error.rs)
```rust
/// Required binary not found on worker.
#[error("Binary '{binary}' not found on worker '{worker_id}' ({host})")]
#[diagnostic(
    code("RCH-E109"),
    help("Deploy the binary with: rch workers deploy-binary {worker_id}")
)]
BinaryNotFound {
    worker_id: String,
    host: String,
    binary: String,
}

/// Toolchain installation failed on worker.
#[error("Toolchain installation failed on '{worker_id}'")]
#[diagnostic(
    code("RCH-E110"),
    help("Check rustup on worker: ssh {user}@{host} rustup show")
)]
ToolchainInstallFailed {
    worker_id: String,
    host: String,
    user: String,
    stderr: String,
}
```

### DaemonError (add to error.rs)
```rust
/// Daemon socket not found.
#[error("Daemon socket not found: {path}")]
#[diagnostic(
    code("RCH-E301"),
    help("Start the daemon with: rch daemon start")
)]
SocketNotFound { path: PathBuf }
```

## Locations to Fix
| Line | Current bail\!() | New Error |
|------|-----------------|-----------|
| 2026 | Generic SSH error | SshError::CommandFailed |
| 2043 | Version check failed | WorkerError::VersionCheckFailed |
| 2167 | rch-wkr not found | WorkerError::BinaryNotFound |
| 2197 | Binary copy failed | WorkerError::DeployFailed |
| 2217 | Ownership change failed | WorkerError::PermissionError |
| 2235 | Chmod failed | WorkerError::PermissionError |
| 2256 | Symlink failed | WorkerError::SymlinkFailed |
| 2943 | rustup install failed | WorkerError::ToolchainInstallFailed |
| 2978 | Missing hostname | ConfigError::MissingEnvVar |
| 5520-5561 | Config validation | ConfigError::InvalidValue |
| 7317 | Socket not found | DaemonError::SocketNotFound |

## Testing
- Each error should have a unit test
- Error codes should be unique
- Error messages should include context (which worker, etc.)
- Help text should be actionable

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/error.rs (add new error variants)
- rch/src/commands.rs (replace bail\!() calls)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:08:05.700801475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:00:14.663257617Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2f8g","depends_on_id":"bd-2u2g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2f8g","depends_on_id":"bd-3b94","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:45Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":14,"issue_id":"bd-2f8g","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (rch/src/error_test.rs)
For EACH new error variant:
1. `test_error_code_format()` - Verify RCH-Exxx format
2. `test_error_has_help_text()` - Verify help() returns non-empty
3. `test_error_includes_context()` - Verify worker_id, host, etc. present
4. `test_error_display_is_user_friendly()` - No internal jargon
5. `test_error_miette_rendering()` - Verify colors, formatting

### Integration Tests
1. Trigger each error condition and verify correct error type
2. Verify JSON output includes error code
3. Verify exit codes match error categories

### E2E Test Script (scripts/test_error_codes.sh)
```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG_FILE=${RCH_TEST_LOG:-/tmp/rch_error_test.log}

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }

# Test 1: Verify all errors have codes
log "Test 1: Checking all errors have RCH-E codes"
rch workers probe nonexistent 2>&1 | grep -q "RCH-E" || {
  log "FAIL: Error missing RCH-E code"
  exit 1
}
log "PASS: Errors have RCH-E codes"

# Test 2: Verify JSON includes error code
log "Test 2: JSON output includes error code"
ERROR_JSON=$(rch workers probe nonexistent --json 2>&1 || true)
if echo "$ERROR_JSON" | jq -e '.error.code' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  log "PASS: JSON includes error code"
else
  log "FAIL: JSON missing error code"
  exit 1
fi

# Test 3: Verify help text present
log "Test 3: Error includes remediation"
rch workers probe nonexistent 2>&1 | grep -qiE "(help|try|verify|check)" || {
  log "FAIL: Error missing remediation hints"
  exit 1
}
log "PASS: Error includes remediation"

log "All error code tests passed"
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log error construction at DEBUG level with all context
- Log error code at ERROR level
- Log remediation attempts at INFO level","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:56:36Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2ga8","title":"True E2E Output Validation & Correctness Tests","description":"# Feature: True E2E Output Validation & Correctness Tests

## Purpose

Test that output from remote compilation (stdout, stderr, exit codes, terminal colors) is correctly preserved and propagated to the calling agent.

## MANDATORY Logging Requirements

Output validation tests MUST use structured JSON logging:

### Output-Specific Log Events

```json
// Output capture start
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_101","phase":"execute","msg":"Command executed","data":{"cmd":"cargo test","mode":"remote","worker":"css"}}

// Exit code verification
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_101","phase":"verify","msg":"Exit code check","data":{"expected":101,"actual":101,"match":true,"semantic":"tests_failed"}}

// stdout/stderr comparison
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_stderr_preservation","phase":"verify","msg":"Output comparison","data":{"stream":"stderr","local_bytes":1234,"remote_bytes":1234,"match":true,"hash_local":"abc123","hash_remote":"abc123"}}

// Color code detection
{"ts":"...","level":"DEBUG","test":"test_ansi_colors","phase":"verify","msg":"ANSI codes detected","data":{"total_codes":15,"colors":["red","green","yellow"],"styles":["bold","underline"]}}

// Streaming latency
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_streaming","phase":"timing","msg":"First byte latency","data":{"local_first_byte_ms":50,"remote_first_byte_ms":85,"overhead_ms":35}}

// Mismatch details (on failure)
{"ts":"...","level":"ERROR","test":"test_output_mismatch","phase":"verify","msg":"Output mismatch detected","data":{"stream":"stdout","line":42,"local":"expected text","remote":"actual text","diff":"<unified diff snippet>"}}
```

### Metrics to Log

1. **Exit codes**: expected, actual, semantic meaning
2. **Output size**: bytes per stream, truncation if any
3. **Content hash**: SHA-256 of local vs remote
4. **ANSI codes**: detected codes, colors, styles
5. **Timing**: first byte latency, total duration
6. **Diff details**: on mismatch, show context

## Why This Matters

- AI agents parse compilation output to understand errors and fix code
- Corrupted or missing output makes agents unable to diagnose issues
- Terminal colors help agents distinguish warnings from errors
- Exit codes determine whether agents retry or report failure

## Output Types to Verify

### Exit Code Semantics

1. **Exit 0** = success (all tests passed, build succeeded)
   - Log: semantic="success", verify stdout contains success indicators
   
2. **Exit 1** = compilation/build error
   - Log: semantic="build_error", verify error message in stderr
   
3. **Exit 101** = cargo test: tests ran but some failed
   - Log: semantic="tests_failed", count failed tests in output
   
4. **Exit 128+N** = killed by signal N
   - Log: signal number, signal name
   
5. **All non-zero** denies local re-execution
   - Log: verify no local fallback on compilation error

### stdout/stderr Preservation

1. **Compiler error messages** (line numbers, context)
   - Log: line number parsed correctly, context preserved
   
2. **Test output** (pass/fail indicators, assertion messages)
   - Log: test count, pass/fail breakdown
   
3. **Warning messages** (not lost or mixed)
   - Log: warning count, no interleaving corruption
   
4. **Progress indicators** (cargo build percentage)
   - Log: progress updates received
   
5. **Multi-line output** (stack traces, long errors)
   - Log: line count, no truncation

### Terminal Formatting

1. **ANSI color codes preserved** (TERM, NO_COLOR, CARGO_TERM_COLOR)
   - Log: TERM value, color codes detected, preservation status
   
2. **Bold/underline for emphasis**
   - Log: style codes found
   
3. **Color-coded test results** (green pass, red fail)
   - Log: pass=green, fail=red verification
   
4. **cargo's progress bar and spinner**
   - Log: carriage returns, spinner frames
   
5. **Wide characters and unicode**
   - Log: unicode points found, correct rendering

### Streaming Behavior

1. **Output streams in real-time** (not batched at end)
   - Log: timestamp of first byte, timestamp of last byte
   
2. **stderr and stdout interleaved correctly**
   - Log: interleaving order preserved
   
3. **Large output (>1MB) handled** without truncation
   - Log: total bytes, no truncation marker
   
4. **Binary data in output** doesn't corrupt stream
   - Log: binary bytes detected, stream integrity

## Merged E2E Scenarios (Hook/Feature-Flag/Error Display)

### Hook Protocol Integrity (from bd-3obh)

1. **Hook JSON response remains pristine**
   - Run: `echo '{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"cargo build"}}' | rch hook --stdin`
   - Verify stdout is valid JSON (no ANSI), stderr only for rich output
   - Log: json_valid=true, ansi_present=false, stderr_bytes

2. **Compilation output passthrough**
   - Run: `rch compile cargo build` on a fixture with known output
   - Verify stdout/stderr identical to local execution (byte-for-byte after normalization)
   - Log: stdout/stderr hashes, diff on mismatch

### Rich Output Gating & Feature Flags (from bd-dej3)

1. **Compile-time rich-ui off**
   - Build without rich-ui feature flag
   - Verify no rich formatting emitted anywhere
   - Log: build_flags, ansi_present=false

2. **Runtime gating**
   - With rich-ui on, ensure `--json` output is still plain JSON and stderr-only rich output
   - With `NO_COLOR=1`, ensure ANSI stripped
   - Log: TERM/NO_COLOR values and ansi detection

### Error Display Verification (from bd-vp67)

1. **SSH connection refused display**
   - Expected: error code, host/port, remediation hint
   - Verify NO_COLOR removes ANSI

2. **Config parse error display**
   - Show file path, line number, and snippet context

3. **Build failure display**
   - Compiler error text preserved verbatim

4. **JSON error mode**

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

  log "FAIL: Duplicate error codes: $DUPES"
  exit 1

log "PASS: No duplicate error codes"

# Check ranges
log "Checking error code ranges"
while read -r code; do
  NUM=${code#RCH-E}
  case $NUM in
    0[0-9][0-9]) CATEGORY="Config" ;;
    1[0-9][0-9]) CATEGORY="SSH/Network" ;;
    2[0-9][0-9]) CATEGORY="Worker" ;;

    4[0-9][0-9]) CATEGORY="Build" ;;
    5[0-9][0-9]) CATEGORY="Hook" ;;
    *) log "WARN: Unknown category for $code"; continue ;;
  esac
  log "OK: $code -> $CATEGORY"
done < /tmp/source_errors.txt

log "Error schema tests complete"
```

 {"id":"bd-3brs","title":"Create RchTheme with color constants and semantic styles","description":"# Create RchTheme with color constants and semantic styles

## Task Description

Create rch-common/src/ui/theme.rs with the RchTheme struct containing all color constants and style generators for consistent visual branding across RCH.

## Background

A unified theme ensures:
1. Visual consistency across all RCH commands
2. Single place to update colors if branding changes
3. Semantic meaning (error = red, success = green)
4. Worker status colors match mental model

## Implementation

### Color Palette

```rust
use rich_rust::prelude::*;

/// RCH brand colors and semantic styles
pub struct RchTheme;

impl RchTheme {
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // BRAND COLORS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Primary brand color - Purple (compilation/build theme)
    pub const PRIMARY: &'static str = "#7C3AED";

    /// Secondary brand color - Cyan (data/transfer theme)
    pub const SECONDARY: &'static str = "#06B6D4";

    /// Accent color - Amber (highlights, attention)
    pub const ACCENT: &'static str = "#F59E0B";

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // SEMANTIC COLORS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Success state - Green
    pub const SUCCESS: &'static str = "#10B981";

    /// Warning state - Amber
    pub const WARNING: &'static str = "#F59E0B";

    /// Error state - Red
    pub const ERROR: &'static str = "#EF4444";

    /// Informational - Blue
    pub const INFO: &'static str = "#3B82F6";

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // WORKER STATUS COLORS
    // These match the WorkerStatus enum from rch-common/types
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Worker is healthy and accepting work
    pub const STATUS_HEALTHY: &'static str = "#10B981";     // Green

    /// Worker is degraded (slow, high error rate)
    pub const STATUS_DEGRADED: &'static str = "#F59E0B";    // Amber

    /// Worker is unreachable (connection failed)
    pub const STATUS_UNREACHABLE: &'static str = "#EF4444"; // Red

    /// Worker is draining (no new work)
    pub const STATUS_DRAINING: &'static str = "#8B5CF6";    // Purple

    /// Worker is disabled (admin disabled)
    pub const STATUS_DISABLED: &'static str = "#6B7280";    // Gray

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // TEXT COLORS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Muted text (secondary information)
    pub const MUTED: &'static str = "#9CA3AF";              // Gray-400

    /// Dim text (tertiary information)
    pub const DIM: &'static str = "#6B7280";                // Gray-500

    /// Bright text (emphasis)
    pub const BRIGHT: &'static str = "#F9FAFB";             // Gray-50

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // STYLE GENERATORS
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Create style for success messages
    pub fn success() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::SUCCESS).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for error messages (bold for emphasis)
    pub fn error() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .bold()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::ERROR).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for warning messages
    pub fn warning() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::WARNING).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for informational messages
    pub fn info() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::INFO).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for muted/secondary text
    pub fn muted() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::MUTED).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for dim/tertiary text
    pub fn dim() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .dim()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::DIM).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for primary brand elements
    pub fn primary() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::PRIMARY).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for secondary brand elements
    pub fn secondary() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::SECONDARY).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Create style for worker status based on WorkerStatus enum
    pub fn worker_status(status: &str) -> Style {
        let color = match status.to_lowercase().as_str() {
            "healthy" => Self::STATUS_HEALTHY,
            "degraded" => Self::STATUS_DEGRADED,
            "unreachable" => Self::STATUS_UNREACHABLE,
            "draining" => Self::STATUS_DRAINING,
            "disabled" => Self::STATUS_DISABLED,
            _ => Self::MUTED,
        };
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(color).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
    // COMPOSITE STYLES
    // ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

    /// Style for table headers
    pub fn table_header() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .bold()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::BRIGHT).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Style for table borders
    pub fn table_border() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::PRIMARY).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Style for panel titles
    pub fn panel_title() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .bold()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::SECONDARY).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Style for command/code text
    pub fn code() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::SECONDARY).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Style for paths/filenames
    pub fn path() -> Style {
        Style::new()
            .italic()
            .color(Color::parse(Self::INFO).unwrap_or_default())
    }

    /// Style for numbers/metrics
    pub fn number() -> Style {
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(Self::ACCENT).unwrap_or_default())
    }
}
```

### Integration with WorkerStatus Type

```rust
// This function should be added or the above adapted
// to work with the actual WorkerStatus enum from rch-common/types
use crate::types::WorkerStatus;

impl RchTheme {
    pub fn for_worker_status(status: WorkerStatus) -> Style {
        let color = match status {
            WorkerStatus::Healthy => Self::STATUS_HEALTHY,
            WorkerStatus::Degraded => Self::STATUS_DEGRADED,
            WorkerStatus::Unreachable => Self::STATUS_UNREACHABLE,
            WorkerStatus::Draining => Self::STATUS_DRAINING,
            WorkerStatus::Disabled => Self::STATUS_DISABLED,
        };
        Style::new().color(Color::parse(color).unwrap_or_default())
    }
}
```

## Color Choice Rationale

### Brand Colors
- **Purple (#7C3AED)**: Compilation/build - sophisticated, technical
- **Cyan (#06B6D4)**: Data transfer - clean, digital
- **Amber (#F59E0B)**: Attention - warm, visible

### Semantic Colors
- **Green (#10B981)**: Success - universal meaning
- **Amber (#F59E0B)**: Warning - caution without alarm
- **Red (#EF4444)**: Error - immediate attention
- **Blue (#3B82F6)**: Info - calm, informational

### Worker Status
- **Green**: Healthy - good to go
- **Amber**: Degraded - working but suboptimal
- **Red**: Unreachable - needs attention
- **Purple**: Draining - intentional state
- **Gray**: Disabled - inactive

## Testing

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_all_colors_parse() {
        // Ensure no color constant is malformed
        assert!(Color::parse(RchTheme::PRIMARY).is_ok());
        assert!(Color::parse(RchTheme::SECONDARY).is_ok());
        assert!(Color::parse(RchTheme::SUCCESS).is_ok());
        assert!(Color::parse(RchTheme::ERROR).is_ok());
        assert!(Color::parse(RchTheme::WARNING).is_ok());
        assert!(Color::parse(RchTheme::INFO).is_ok());
        assert!(Color::parse(RchTheme::STATUS_HEALTHY).is_ok());
        // ... etc for all constants
    }

    #[test]
```

 {"id":"bd-3c8d","title":"E2E Test Script: Self-Healing Validation","description":"# E2E Test Script: Self-Healing Validation

## Overview
A comprehensive end-to-end test script that validates the entire self-healing system works correctly in real-world scenarios. This script should be runnable by developers and CI to ensure the self-healing features work as designed.

## Test Script Location
`tests/e2e/test_self_healing.sh`

## Script Structure

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# =============================================================================
# E2E Test: RCH Self-Healing System
# =============================================================================
#
# This script validates the mutually reinforcing self-healing behavior:
# 1. Hook auto-starts daemon when unavailable
# 2. Daemon auto-installs hooks on startup
# 3. Doctor --fix repairs both
#
# Prerequisites:
# - rch and rchd binaries in PATH
# - Write access to ~/.claude/ and /tmp/
# - No production daemon running (test uses isolated config)
#
# Usage:
#   ./tests/e2e/test_self_healing.sh [--verbose]
#

# =============================================================================
# Configuration
# =============================================================================

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
TEST_HOME=$(mktemp -d)
TEST_CONFIG_DIR="${TEST_HOME}/.config/rch"
TEST_CLAUDE_DIR="${TEST_HOME}/.claude"
TEST_SOCKET="/tmp/rch-test-$$.sock"
LOG_FILE="${TEST_HOME}/test.log"

VERBOSE="${1:-}"
PASSED=0
FAILED=0
TOTAL=0

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# =============================================================================
# Logging Functions
# =============================================================================

log() {
    local level=$1
    shift
    local timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%3N')
    echo -e "[$timestamp] [$level] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_info()  { log "INFO " "$*"; }
log_pass()  { log "${GREEN}PASS${NC} " "$*"; }
log_fail()  { log "${RED}FAIL${NC} " "$*"; }
log_test()  { log "${BLUE}TEST${NC} " "$*"; }
log_detail() { [[ "$VERBOSE" == "--verbose" ]] && log "DEBUG" "$*" || true; }

# =============================================================================
# Test Utilities
# =============================================================================

setup_test_env() {
    log_info "Setting up isolated test environment..."
    log_detail "TEST_HOME=$TEST_HOME"
    
    mkdir -p "$TEST_CONFIG_DIR"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_CLAUDE_DIR"
    
    # Create minimal config pointing to test socket
    cat > "${TEST_CONFIG_DIR}/config.toml" <<EOF
socket_path = "${TEST_SOCKET}"

[self_healing]
hook_starts_daemon = true
daemon_installs_hooks = true
daemon_start_timeout = 5
auto_start_cooldown = 5
EOF
    
    export HOME="$TEST_HOME"
    export RCH_CONFIG="${TEST_CONFIG_DIR}/config.toml"
    export RCH_SOCKET_PATH="$TEST_SOCKET"
    
    log_info "Test environment ready"
}

cleanup() {
    log_info "Cleaning up test environment..."
    
    # Stop any test daemon
    if [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_detail "Stopping test daemon..."
        rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
    
    # Kill any stray rchd processes from this test
    pkill -f "rchd.*${TEST_SOCKET}" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Remove test directory
    if [[ -d "$TEST_HOME" && "$TEST_HOME" == /tmp/* ]]; then
        rm -rf "$TEST_HOME"
    fi
    
    log_info "Cleanup complete"
}

trap cleanup EXIT

assert_eq() {
    local expected=$1
    local actual=$2
    local msg=$3
    
    if [[ "$expected" == "$actual" ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "Assertion failed: $msg"
        log_detail "  Expected: $expected"
        log_detail "  Actual:   $actual"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_file_exists() {
    local file=$1
    local msg=$2
    
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "File not found: $file ($msg)"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_socket_exists() {
    local socket=$1
    local msg=$2
    
    if [[ -S "$socket" ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "Socket not found: $socket ($msg)"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_contains() {
    local haystack=$1
    local needle=$2
    local msg=$3
    
    if [[ "$haystack" == *"$needle"* ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "String not found: '$needle' ($msg)"
        log_detail "  In: $haystack"
        return 1
    fi
}

run_test() {
    local name=$1
    shift
    local test_fn=$1
    
    TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
    log_test "Running: $name"
    
    if $test_fn; then
        PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
        log_pass "$name"
        return 0
    else
        FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
        log_fail "$name"
        return 1
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# Test Cases
# =============================================================================

test_hook_auto_starts_daemon() {
    log_detail "Ensuring daemon is stopped..."
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    sleep 1
    
    # Verify daemon not running
    if [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Precondition failed: daemon still running"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Creating test project..."
    local test_project="${TEST_HOME}/test_project"
    mkdir -p "$test_project"
    cat > "${test_project}/Cargo.toml" <<EOF
[package]
name = "test_project"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
EOF
    mkdir -p "${test_project}/src"
    echo 'fn main() { println!("Hello"); }' > "${test_project}/src/main.rs"
    
    log_detail "Invoking hook (should auto-start daemon)..."
    local hook_input='{"event":"PreToolUse","tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"cargo build --release"},"session_id":"test","session_cwd":"'"$test_project"'"}'
    
    local output
    output=$(echo "$hook_input" | timeout 30 rch 2>&1) || true
    
    log_detail "Hook output: $output"
    
    # Check if daemon was auto-started
    sleep 2
    if ! [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Daemon was not auto-started"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Daemon auto-started successfully"
    return 0
}

test_daemon_auto_installs_hook() {
    log_detail "Removing existing hook..."
    rm -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json"
    
    log_detail "Stopping daemon..."
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    sleep 1
    
    log_detail "Starting daemon (should install hook)..."
    rch daemon start
    sleep 2
    
    # Check if hook was installed
    if ! assert_file_exists "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" "Hook settings file should exist"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Verify hook content
    local settings
    settings=$(cat "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json")
    
    if ! assert_contains "$settings" '"command": "rch"' "Settings should contain rch hook"; then
        log_detail "Settings content: $settings"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Hook auto-installed successfully"
    return 0
}

test_daemon_preserves_existing_hooks() {
    log_detail "Creating settings with existing DCG hook..."
    mkdir -p "$TEST_CLAUDE_DIR"
    cat > "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" <<EOF
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [
      {
        "command": "dcg",
        "description": "Destructive Command Guard"
      }
    ]
  }
}
EOF
    
    log_detail "Restarting daemon..."
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    sleep 1
    rch daemon start
    sleep 2
    
    # Verify both hooks present
    local settings
    settings=$(cat "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json")
    
    if ! assert_contains "$settings" '"command": "dcg"' "DCG hook should be preserved"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    if ! assert_contains "$settings" '"command": "rch"' "RCH hook should be added"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Existing hooks preserved successfully"
    return 0
}

test_doctor_fix_installs_hook() {
    log_detail "Removing hook..."
    rm -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json"
    
    log_detail "Running doctor --fix..."
    local output
    output=$(rch doctor --fix 2>&1)
    
    log_detail "Doctor output: $output"
    
    if ! assert_file_exists "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" "Doctor should install hook"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    if ! assert_contains "$output" "Fixed" "Doctor should report fix"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Doctor --fix installed hook successfully"
    return 0
}

test_doctor_fix_starts_daemon() {
    log_detail "Stopping daemon..."
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    sleep 1
    
    log_detail "Running doctor --fix..."
    local output
    output=$(rch doctor --fix 2>&1)
    
    log_detail "Doctor output: $output"
    
    sleep 2
    if ! assert_socket_exists "$TEST_SOCKET" "Doctor should start daemon"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Doctor --fix started daemon successfully"
    return 0
}

test_doctor_dry_run_no_changes() {
    log_detail "Stopping daemon and removing hook..."
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    rm -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json"
    sleep 1
    
    log_detail "Running doctor --fix --dry-run..."
    local output
    output=$(rch doctor --fix --dry-run 2>&1)
    
    log_detail "Doctor output: $output"
    
    # Verify no changes made
    if [[ -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" ]]; then
        log_fail "Dry run should not create settings file"
        return 1
    fi
    
    if [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Dry run should not start daemon"
        return 1
    fi
    
    if ! assert_contains "$output" "Would" "Dry run should say 'Would'"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Dry run made no changes as expected"
    return 0
}

test_auto_start_cooldown() {
    log_detail "This test validates cooldown prevents restart spam"
    
    # Start and immediately stop daemon
    rch daemon start
    sleep 1
    rch daemon stop
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    
    # Record time
    local start_time=$(date +%s)
    
    # Try to trigger auto-start via hook
    local test_project="${TEST_HOME}/test_project2"
    mkdir -p "$test_project/src"
    cat > "${test_project}/Cargo.toml" <<EOF
[package]
name = "test_project2"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
EOF
    echo 'fn main() {}' > "${test_project}/src/main.rs"
    
    local hook_input='{"event":"PreToolUse","tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"cargo build"},"session_id":"test","session_cwd":"'"$test_project"'"}'
    
    log_detail "First hook invocation..."
    echo "$hook_input" | timeout 10 rch 2>&1 || true
    
    # Check if cooldown file was created
    local cooldown_file="/tmp/rch-autostart-cooldown"
    if [[ -f "$cooldown_file" ]]; then
        log_detail "Cooldown file created"
    fi
    
    log_detail "Second hook invocation (should skip due to cooldown)..."
    local output
    output=$(echo "$hook_input" | timeout 5 rch 2>&1) || true
    
    # The second invocation should be fast (not waiting for daemon)
    # because cooldown prevents restart attempt
    local end_time=$(date +%s)
    local elapsed=$((end_time - start_time))
    
    log_detail "Elapsed time: ${elapsed}s"
    
    # If cooldown works, second invocation should be quick
    # (This is a heuristic test - timing-based)
    return 0
}

test_config_disables_self_healing() {
    log_detail "Creating config with self-healing disabled..."
    cat > "${TEST_CONFIG_DIR}/config.toml" <<EOF
socket_path = "${TEST_SOCKET}"

[self_healing]
hook_starts_daemon = false
daemon_installs_hooks = false
EOF
    
    # Stop daemon and remove hook
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    rm -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json"
    sleep 1
    
    log_detail "Starting daemon (should NOT install hook)..."
    rch daemon start
    sleep 2
    
    # Hook should NOT be installed (disabled in config)
    if [[ -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" ]]; then
        local settings
        settings=$(cat "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json")
        if [[ "$settings" == *'"command": "rch"'* ]]; then
            log_fail "Hook was installed despite being disabled in config"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    
    log_detail "Self-healing correctly disabled by config"
    
    # Restore default config
    cat > "${TEST_CONFIG_DIR}/config.toml" <<EOF
socket_path = "${TEST_SOCKET}"

[self_healing]
hook_starts_daemon = true
daemon_installs_hooks = true
EOF
    
    return 0
}

test_full_self_healing_cycle() {
    log_detail "=== Full Self-Healing Cycle Test ==="
    
    # Start from completely clean state
    log_detail "Step 1: Clean state"
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    rm -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json"
    rm -rf "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}"
    sleep 1
    
    # Verify nothing exists
    if [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]] || [[ -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" ]]; then
        log_fail "Clean state not achieved"
        return 1
    fi
    log_detail "  ✓ Clean state verified"
    
    # Run doctor --fix (should fix everything)
    log_detail "Step 2: Run doctor --fix"
    local output
    output=$(rch doctor --fix 2>&1)
    log_detail "Doctor output: $output"
    sleep 2
    
    # Verify hook installed
    if ! [[ -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" ]]; then
        log_fail "Hook not installed by doctor --fix"
        return 1
    fi
    log_detail "  ✓ Hook installed"
    
    # Verify daemon running
    if ! [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Daemon not started by doctor --fix"
        return 1
    fi
    log_detail "  ✓ Daemon running"
    
    # Simulate daemon crash
    log_detail "Step 3: Simulate daemon crash"
    pkill -f "rchd.*${TEST_SOCKET}" 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    sleep 1
    
    if [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Daemon still running after simulated crash"
        return 1
    fi
    log_detail "  ✓ Daemon crashed (simulated)"
    
    # Trigger build (hook should auto-restart daemon)
    log_detail "Step 4: Trigger build (should auto-restart daemon)"
    local test_project="${TEST_HOME}/test_project_cycle"
    mkdir -p "$test_project/src"
    cat > "${test_project}/Cargo.toml" <<EOF
[package]
name = "cycle_test"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
EOF
    echo 'fn main() { println!("cycle test"); }' > "${test_project}/src/main.rs"
    
    local hook_input='{"event":"PreToolUse","tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"cargo build --release"},"session_id":"cycle-test","session_cwd":"'"$test_project"'"}'
    
    output=$(echo "$hook_input" | timeout 30 rch 2>&1) || true
    log_detail "Hook output: $output"
    sleep 2
    
    # Verify daemon auto-restarted
    if ! [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Daemon not auto-restarted by hook"
        return 1
    fi
    log_detail "  ✓ Daemon auto-restarted"
    
    log_detail "=== Full cycle complete ==="
    return 0
}

# =============================================================================
# Main
# =============================================================================

main() {
    echo ""
    echo "=============================================="
    echo "  RCH Self-Healing E2E Test Suite"
    echo "=============================================="
    echo ""
    
    setup_test_env
    
    # Run all tests
    run_test "Hook auto-starts daemon" test_hook_auto_starts_daemon
    run_test "Daemon auto-installs hook" test_daemon_auto_installs_hook
    run_test "Daemon preserves existing hooks" test_daemon_preserves_existing_hooks
    run_test "Doctor --fix installs hook" test_doctor_fix_installs_hook
    run_test "Doctor --fix starts daemon" test_doctor_fix_starts_daemon
    run_test "Doctor --dry-run makes no changes" test_doctor_dry_run_no_changes
    run_test "Auto-start cooldown works" test_auto_start_cooldown
    run_test "Config disables self-healing" test_config_disables_self_healing
    run_test "Full self-healing cycle" test_full_self_healing_cycle
    
    # Summary
    echo ""
    echo "=============================================="
    echo "  Test Results"
    echo "=============================================="
    echo ""
    echo -e "  Total:  $TOTAL"
    echo -e "  ${GREEN}Passed: $PASSED${NC}"
    echo -e "  ${RED}Failed: $FAILED${NC}"
    echo ""
    
    if [[ $FAILED -gt 0 ]]; then
        echo -e "${RED}SOME TESTS FAILED${NC}"
        echo "See $LOG_FILE for details"
        exit 1
    else
        echo -e "${GREEN}ALL TESTS PASSED${NC}"
        exit 0
    fi
}

main "$@"
```
